protocol: medical - supraventricular tachycardia (including atrial fibrillation) medical - tachycardia medical - ventricular tachycardia with a pulse (protocol 2 - 8)

dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for heart problems at 123 Redacted Drive.
Hi, I'm Jordan, an EMT with the rescue squad. My partner is Raj. We want to help you feel better. Is it okay if we take a look?
Yeah... Uh, my heart is racing. I have a feeling it's been going real fast since this morning.
Mr. Smith, can you tell me your name for me?
Sure, it's Martin. My birthday is June 12, 1957.
Great. What county are we in?
We're in [CITY] County.
A&O x4: Mr. Smith, what month and year is it?
December 8th, 2019.
What happened earlier today?
I was doing some house cleaning and then all of a sudden I could feel my heart beat so fast.
Great to know. Can you tell me who's with you right now?
I'm with my wife in the living room.
Airway is clear and patent. We're good for now.
Mr. Smith, we're going to observe your respiratory rate now.
I'm pretty comfortable, just a little lightheaded at times.
I'm counting your breaths. You're breathing 24 times a minute. That's faster than normal.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Equal bilateral chest rise and fall
dialogue.utterance: Your chest is rising and falling equally on both sides.

dialogue.micro_intent: check circulation - Pulse rate and quality
dialogue.utterance: Radial pulse is strong and regular. You have a good circulation.
Mr. Smith, your heart rate is 194 beats per minute.
Your blood pressure is 115 over 80. That stays within safe limits for now.
Your airway, breathing, and circulation are all currently stable.
Mr. Smith, can you talk with us right now?
Yes, I can tell you what's going on.
What is your name?
It's Martin Smith.
Do you know where you are?
I'm in my house in [CITY].
Mr. Smith, can you tell me today's date, who you're with, and where you are?
Yes, December 8th in [CITY], and you're the EMTs.
I notice you're moving your limbs normally. Positive PMS.
You mentioned your heart has been racing. Have you had any chest pain or pain in other areas?
No chest pain or other pain, just dizziness.
Any other pain or discomfort? On a scale from 0 to 10?
No, pain is 0, but the racing heart is really intense.
We're going to start an IV line to help us treat you quickly if needed. This will be in your arm, okay?
You look a bit perspiring. Is it unusual for you?
Yeah, a little more than usual.
Your skin is warm aside from the sweating.
You mentioned your heart has been racing. When exactly did it start?
Around 4:15am while I was cleaning the kitchen. It didn't slow down no matter what I tried.
Can you rank the intensity of the discomfort on a scale from 0 to 10?
It's not painful per se but definitely seven or eight. Like my heart never rests.
Has it gotten any better or worse relative to now?
It feels steady, maybe a little less intense now, not better though.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Have you had something like this before? How often?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Yeah, about a month ago, I had the same racing heart episode and had a doctor's appointment in February.
What medical history do you have?
I have a history of diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and supraventricular tachycardia, or SVT.
Are you currently under treatment for any of these?
Sir, can you tell us what medications you're taking now?
I take Metoprolol, Atorvastatin, Lisinopril, Metformin, Methotrexate, Folic Acid, and Aspirin daily.
Have you made any recent lifestyle or medication adjustments?
No, I'm on those for years. Tried vagal maneuvers, but they weren't working.
The EKG tracing shows supraventricular tachycardia. Let's get ready for treatment.
Your heart rate is much higher than it should be for someone in your condition. I want to give you some medicine to slow it down safely. Is that okay?
Yeah, please do whatever you need to.
Administering 6 mg of Adenosine now to help reduce the heart rate.
Heart rhythm is shifting to sinus with a declining rate to 92 beats per minute.
Blood pressure has slightly increased, and your breathing is stabilizing. Resp is 18 now.
SPO2 is at 98%. Heart rate is 97, and your BP is 129 over 77.
Your vitals are now a safer, more stable range.
Just to clarify, there's no chest or body pain at all?
Correct, it's just the racing heart.
Performing 4-lead EKG monitoring now. It helps track your heart rhythm during transport.
About to perform a 12-lead EKG to get a more detailed view of your heart's activity.
The 12-lead EKG is complete and shows SVT but no other acute concerns right now.
Let's reassess again in a few minutes to track your progress.
Your skin is still perspiring but that's expected.
Your skin remains warm.
We will get you safely down the steps and into our ambulance.
Once we get you onboard, I'll strap you in for a smooth ride. You're going to be fine.
I wanted to know which hospital you prefer, sir?
[COMPANY_NAME] is fine for me. That's the one I'm used to seeing.
Great, we'll head to [COMPANY_NAME] for more monitoring and care.
Everyone, let's get the stretcher out and we can move you down the stairs gently.
Mr. Smith, we got you on the stretcher, strapped in, and ready to roll.
[COMPANY_NAME], we are en route with Mr. Smith, a 62-year-old male. Heart rate started at SVT, managed with Adenosine, now sinus rhythm and stable.
IV access was established, NS at KVO, and EKGs were all properly done.
Medication documentation and PCR are complete and in order.
Left the drug box, gave pharmacist my signature.
Passed PCR to ED and signed off with the RN for tracking. All notes are properly updated.
Mr. Smith is alert and oriented times four with no signs of pain.
He has a sinus rhythm now and has been stable during transport.
We've given him Adenosine, stabilized and supported during transport.
It's been a smooth transport and he is in good condition.
Any further questions from the team?
Then that's all from our side. Continue the monitoring plan and handle with care.
